Title: A 2-oxoglutarate-dependent dioxygenase converts dihydrofuran to furan in Salvia diterpenoids

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: 2-oxoglutarate-dependent dioxygenase (2-ODD), DOXC31/D4H-BX6 clade; contains DIOX_N and 2OG-FeII_Oxy domains
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Dihydrofuran- to furan-tanshinone branch; furan ring formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Dehydrogenates/desaturates the dihydrofuran D-ring by introducing a double bond between C15 and C16, converting cryptotanshinone to tanshinone IIA and iCT to isotanshinone IIA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a bottleneck/key control step controlling flux from dihydrofuran- to furan-tanshinones
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive medicinal constituent; furano-quinone moiety contributes substantially to anti-cancer activity and DNA minor-groove binding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of furan-tanshinones including tanshinone IIA and reduces dihydrofuran-tanshinones; RNAi causes decreased TIIA and increased cryptotanshinone, methylenedihydrotanshinquinone, and DTI
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in root; much lower in aerial tissues, nearly undetectable in stem and inflorescence; induced by ABA and SA and slightly by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in root; much lower in aerial tissues, nearly undetectable in stem and inflorescence; induced by ABA and SA and slightly by MeJA in hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA and SA treatment increased expression; MeJA had limited induction; optimal in vitro activity at 20°C and pH 6.5; requires 2-oxoglutarate and is facilitated by Fe2+ and ascorbate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Differential transcriptome mining; heterologous expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay with authentic standards and LC-MS/Q Exactive; substrate specificity assay; RT-qPCR tissue expression; RNAi silencing in hairy roots with metabolite profiling; kinetic analysis; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi shifts metabolic flux toward accumulation of dihydrofuran-tanshinones and reduces furan-tanshinones; other growth/yield penalties not reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for metabolic engineering to increase pharmacologically valuable furan-tanshinones such as TIIA; likely beneficial in pathway engineering/stacking with upstream tanshinone pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Strict substrate specificity: active on cryptotanshinone and iCT, inactive toward methylenedihydrotanshinquinone, DTI, tetrahydrotanshinone I, and 1,2-didehydrocryptotanshinone. First enzyme characterized to dehydrogenate a dihydrofuran ring to a furan in this pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sm2-ODD14 was identified from root-enriched 2-ODD candidates as the only enzyme converting cryptotanshinone to tanshinone IIA in vitro. 2. The enzyme, designated SmTIIAS, catalyzes aromatization/desaturation of the dihydrofuran ring and directly forms the furan ring-containing product. 3. SmTIIAS is root-enriched and induced by ABA/SA, matching tanshinone accumulation patterns. 4. RNAi repression decreased tanshinone IIA and tanshinone I while increasing cryptotanshinone and other dihydrofuran-tanshinones, demonstrating in planta control of metabolic flux. 5. SmTIIAS functions as a bottleneck step controlling conversion from dihydrofuran- to furan-tanshinones.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: 2-oxoglutarate-dependent dioxygenase (2-ODD), DOXC31/D4H-BX6 clade; 2-His-1-carboxylate facial triad and 2OG-binding residues
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts cryptotanshinone to tanshinone IIA by dehydrogenating/aromatizing the dihydrofuran ring
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Most effective lipophilic medicinal ingredient in S. miltiorrhiza; associated with anti-cancer activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro assay and RNAi in hairy root
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering target to enhance TIIA production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SmTIIAS is the designated name of Sm2-ODD14; same core gene
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmTIIAS is Sm2-ODD14. 2. It directly synthesizes TIIA from CT. 3. It controls flux to furan-tanshinones.
